shadows.name: hero-vignette
shadows.purpose: The only structural 'shadow' — a low vignette that deepens the hero's edges. The system is otherwise flat.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Reveals and hovers — exponential ease-out, no bounce.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single primary CTA per view — carries the page's one goal.

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Secondary action — transparent with amber text and border.

components.name: Underline-Draw Link
components.description: Default feature/nav link — an amber underline scales in from the left on hover.

components.name: Email Input
components.description: The email-only newsletter field; border warms to amber on focus.

components.name: Card
components.description: Flat dark card defined by a hairline; warms its border and lifts 2px on hover.
components.html:
Fiction

Novels and stories, warm at the center and darker at the edges.

narrative.overview: A warm-near-black world, lit by one amber light, with the cold pressing at the edges. Cinematic without being loud, literary without being a magazine pastiche, intimate without being precious. Restrained on purpose but never timid — it earns its negative space the way a fine hardback earns wide margins, as confidence, not emptiness. Surfaces are flat and dark, defined by hairlines rather than shadows; a single amber accent does all the emotional work and is rationed so its rarity stays meaningful. Atmosphere (faint grain, warm-to-cold gradient, low vignette) runs full-bleed across the whole device, while the text stays in a calm, readable column.
keyCharacteristics: Warm near-black surfaces; one rationed amber accent; the cold kept to the corners.
One characterful serif (Fraunces) + one clean sans (Inter); slogan stated once.
Flat by default — depth from hairlines, tonal layering, grain, and one warm light source.
Full-bleed atmosphere, calm capped text column; restraint that reads as confidence.
Refined, barely-there motion; reduced-motion fully honored.

rules.name: The One Amber Rule
rules.body: Amber is an accent, never a surface. Single primary button, links, marks, focus rings — never a large fill. Its rarity is the point.

rules.name: The Cold-Corner Rule
rules.body: The cold blue-grey is darker than the room it sits in — a shadow that eats light, confined to a corner or edge. Never a glowing wash, never a stripe, never the whole field.

rules.name: The Raincoat Rule
rules.body: #E8C547 is forbidden anywhere except the novel's own contexts.

rules.name: The Slogan-Once Rule
rules.body: 'Warm stories with cold corners.' is the hero H1 and appears nowhere else on the site.

rules.name: The Readable-Measure Rule
rules.body: The frame may breathe wide on large screens, but a text column never exceeds ~75 characters. The frame scales; the measure does not.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default / Hairline Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest, separated by hairlines and tonal steps. Never pair a 1px border with a soft wide drop shadow.

dos: Do light every key surface with one warm source and one cold corner — warmth for trust, shadow for intrigue.
Do keep one primary CTA ('Read the first pages') threaded through hero, mid-page, and footer; demote everything else to quiet links.
Do ration amber to accent only (#C9A24B); carry warmth with type, light, and imagery instead.
Do cap body text at 65-75 characters even as the frame widens; run atmosphere full-bleed and scale up type/spacing on large screens.
Do keep elevation flat — hairlines, tonal layering, grain; lift 2-3px and warm the border on hover.
Do describe, never label; keep everything spoiler-safe; state the slogan exactly once.
Do honor prefers-reduced-motion for every animation, and verify WCAG AA contrast on every new text/background pair.
donts: Don't ship anything that could read as AI-generated or templated — the cardinal sin.
Don't use generic cafe-brown/coffee-shop browns, neon, corporate blue, or sweet pastels.
Don't add a cookie banner, exit-intent popup, or any pushy interrupt — the calm is the trust signal.
Don't default to editorial-magazine costume (drop-caps + broadsheet grid) on a brief that isn't a magazine.
Don't fake rain or texture with diagonal repeating-linear-gradient stripes — they read as cheap lines, not weather.
Don't pair a 1px border with a soft wide drop shadow; don't over-round (cards stay 2-3px); don't use border-left/right >1px as a colored stripe.
Don't let raincoat yellow (#E8C547) appear outside novel contexts; don't let amber become a large fill.
Don't put the unrevealed book cover on the homepage — it's a subscriber reward, not free decoration.
